summary: Completed testing of broadcast history feature for German dating app Eros. Backend APIs are fully functional, but frontend has authentication/session persistence issues preventing full UI testing.

fixed_issues.impact: Caused 500 errors affecting account page loading

critical_bugs.issue: User session does not persist after login - redirects back to login page when accessing /account
critical_bugs.impact: Prevents access to account page where BroadcastHistorySection is located

integration_issues.issue: Cannot test BroadcastHistorySection UI due to authentication issues

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/me/broadcasts (basic) - returns correct structure with broadcasts, total, limit, skip, has_more
Backend: GET /api/me/broadcasts with severity filter (info) - filters correctly
Backend: GET /api/me/broadcasts with read_status filter (unread) - filters correctly
Backend: GET /api/me/broadcasts with include_expired=true - includes expired broadcasts
Backend: GET /api/me/broadcasts with search filter - searches in title and body
Backend: GET /api/me/broadcasts with pagination (skip/limit) - paginates correctly
Backend: GET /api/me/broadcasts with date filter (since) - filters by date
Backend: GET /api/me/broadcasts backward compatibility (unread_only=true, limit=10) - maintains compatibility
Backend: POST /api/me/broadcasts/{id}/ack - marks individual broadcast as read
Backend: POST /api/me/broadcasts/ack-all - marks all broadcasts as read, returns {ok, marked}
Backend: Admin broadcast creation - creates test broadcasts successfully
Frontend: BroadcastHistorySection component exists and is imported in AccountPage.js
Frontend: All required data-testid attributes are present in component code
Frontend: Component structure includes search, filters, actions, and pagination

success_percentage.frontend: 0% (0/10 tests completed due to authentication issues)

action_item_for_main_agent: Fix frontend authentication/session persistence issue preventing access to /account page. The BroadcastHistorySection component is properly implemented but cannot be tested due to login session not persisting. User gets redirected to login page when trying to access /account.
